items.title: Record-breaking Ecclestone helps England dismiss India for 285 at Lord's
items.summary: Sophie Ecclestone became England Women's leading wicket-taker across all formats as the hosts dismissed India for 285 on a see-saw opening day of the historic first women's Test at Lord's.

items.title: Major blow for Belgium as Premier League star is INJURED in warm-up and replaced last minute for Spain World Cup clash
items.summary: YOURI TIELEMANS has withdrawn in warm ups as Belgium are dealt major blow ahead of World Cup quarter final clash. The Aston Villa star has been replaced in the midfield by Hans Vanaken as Belgium look to overcome Spain. Belgium have gone from strength to strength in the tournament since their draws

items.title: Man Utd’s £35m Ederson transfer in serious DOUBT with medical still not done and fears deal could collapse
items.summary: EDERSON’S £35million move to Manchester United is in severe doubt. United agreed a deal with Atalanta in early June and expected to finalise the Brazilian’s signing this month. But United have still not conducted a full medical on the 26-year-old, raising doubts as to whether the deal will go throug

items.title: Cristiano Ronaldo reunited with old manager who helped him lift league title as Portugal announce Martinez replacement
items.summary: JORGE JESUS will be the new Portugal head coach as he looks forward to a Cristiano Ronaldo reunion. The Al-Nassr boss has been appointed to the role on a four-year deal that will run until the 2030 World Cup. Roberto Martinez stepped down from the role following the nation's 1-0 defeat to Spain in t

items.title: Mo Salah’s salary demands risk scuppering dream reunion in Saudi Arabia as Liverpool icon seeks free transfer
items.summary: MOHAMED SALAH continues to hold talks over a move to Saudi Arabia but his financial demands are currently proving to be too high. items.title: Man City's Lewis on faith, football and helping the homeless
items.summary: Manchester City midfielder Rico Lewis talks about finding God and his charity work involving football that is aiming to tackle homelessness.

items.title: Djokovic: I want to return to Wimbledon at least one more time
items.summary: Novak Djokovic says he wants to return to Wimbledon "at least one more time" and does not feel any pressure to continue his tennis career.

items.title: Can King Arthur conquer America? Fery eyes US Open after Wimbledon heroics
items.summary: Will we see a fairytale of New York as new British No 1 Arthur Fery heads to the US Open? Watch every match live on Sky Sports from August 30 to September 13.

items.title: Burnley appoint Hayen as new head coach
items.summary: Burnley appoint Genk boss Nicky Hayen as their new head coach on a three-year deal.

items.title: New species named after Cape Verde keeper Vozinha
items.summary: A new species of sea snail has been named after Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha to mark his performance against Spain at the World Cup.

items.title: Players and staff to reap £19m bonus bonanza if England win World Cup
items.summary: FA would pay out half its £38m Fifa prize money Players could net £15m, Thomas Tuchel £3m England’s players and coaching staff will receive around half of the FA’s £38m Fifa prize money in bonuses if they win the World Cup. items.summary: WORLD CUP organisers were slammed by the BBC commentary team during Spain vs Belgium.
